Interactive Resources

Tools & Explainers

Not slides. Not PDFs. Each tool is interactive — built to make abstract healthcare data concepts concrete and learnable.

LAB
Lab section active — Some tools are in active development and locked while under construction. Visit the Lab to see what’s coming.
Visit the Lab →
Live Tools
Interactive Map
AI
AI Healthcare Map
A layered, clickable diagram of how AI intersects the full healthcare system — from the bedside to the billing department. Know what actually works, what is emerging, and what is still hype.
AIStrategyInteractive
→
Game
SQL
Clinical SQL Mystery
Learn the Patient → Encounter → Clinical Event data model by solving a real clinical mystery using SQL queries against an Epic-style database. Four cases, escalating difficulty. No experience needed.
SQLEHRBeginner-friendly
→
Explainer
Ev
Event vs. Request vs. Observation
The three fundamental building blocks of clinical data in FHIR and EHR systems — and why confusing them causes real problems in reporting, billing, and AI model training.
FHIRData ModelClinical
→
In Development — Lab
Lab Preview
Gap
U.S. Healthcare Gap Map
An interactive D3.js choropleth map of U.S. healthcare resource inequality — workforce shortages, rural-urban divides, ESRD prevalence, and state-level disparity data. Currently in development.
D3.jsDisparitiesState Data
In Lab — locked
Lab Preview
4P
4Ps System Map
The full layered workflow diagram of the healthcare system — Patient, Provider, Payer, and Policy Maker lanes with clickable nodes linking to tools and articles. The navigational spine of this site.
D3.js4PsNavigation
In Lab — locked
Reference
Ref
HIT Terminology Reference
Patient IDs, encounter identifiers, clinical events, and how they connect across Epic, Cerner, and FHIR. A plain-English glossary sourced from public documentation.
HL7FHIRGlossary
→
A living resource for clinical informatics, healthcare data architecture, and the real story behind the systems that run modern medicine.

Built by David Eitel, RRT, MHA, MSRT, RRT-ACCS

Active development
Source Policy
All vendor-specific terminology, technical specifications, and system references on this site are derived from publicly available documentation — including fhir.epic.com, fhir.cerner.com, HL7 FHIR R4, ONC HealthIT.gov, and HHS.gov. Each factual claim is linked to its source. This site does not reproduce proprietary materials, internal vendor training content, or licensed documentation.
